seoDescription: Learn California's $500,000 economic nexus threshold for remote sellers. Complete guide to CA sales tax registration, filing frequency, and compliance.
keyDetails: California uses a $500,000 sales-only threshold — there is no separate transaction count threshold.
The threshold is based on total sales into California, including exempt and marketplace sales.
Marketplace facilitators like Amazon are required to collect on behalf of sellers for marketplace sales.
District taxes (local surtaxes) apply in addition to the 7.25% base state rate and vary by jurisdiction.

seoDescription: Understand Texas's $500,000 economic nexus threshold. Complete guide to TX sales tax permits, quarterly filing, and compliance for remote sellers.
keyDetails: Texas uses a $500,000 revenue-only threshold with no separate transaction count requirement.
The threshold is measured over the preceding 12 calendar months of total Texas revenue.
Texas has no state income tax, making sales tax the primary revenue collection method.
Local jurisdictions can add up to 2% on top of the 6.25% state rate, for a max combined rate of 8.25%.

seoDescription: New York's economic nexus requires $500,000 in sales AND 100 transactions. Full guide to NY sales tax registration and compliance for remote sellers.
keyDetails: New York uses a dual threshold: you must exceed BOTH $500,000 in sales AND 100 transactions to trigger nexus.
This is one of the highest thresholds in the country, so smaller sellers may not need to register.
The state rate is 4%, but combined local rates can push the total to 8.875% in New York City.
New York requires a Certificate of Authority before you can legally collect sales tax.

seoDescription: Florida's $100,000 economic nexus threshold explained. Learn about FL sales tax registration, filing schedules, and compliance requirements for remote sellers.
keyDetails: Florida adopted economic nexus relatively late (July 2021) with a $100,000 threshold based on prior-year sales.
There is no transaction count threshold — only the dollar amount matters.
Filing frequency depends on your tax liability: monthly for larger sellers, quarterly for smaller ones.
Local discretionary sales surtaxes can add 0.5%–2.5% on top of the 6% state rate, varying by county.

seoDescription: Illinois requires registration at $100,000 in sales OR 200 transactions. Full guide to IL economic nexus, Retailers' Occupation Tax, and compliance.
keyDetails: Illinois uses an OR threshold: exceeding EITHER $100,000 in sales OR 200 transactions triggers nexus.
This is one of the lowest thresholds nationally for transaction-based nexus, catching many mid-size sellers.
Illinois has a complex tax structure with the Retailers' Occupation Tax (ROT) as its primary sales tax.
Home-rule municipalities can impose additional taxes, and combined rates can exceed 10% in Chicago.
